title: Convex-polytope structure and exposed-edge identification of the companion family

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Establish the structural facts the edge/vertex argument relies on:
(1) $\mathcal A=\{A(p):p\in\mathcal P\}$ is the affine image of the hyperbox $\prod_i[a_i^-,a_i^+]$ under $a\mapsto A_0-e_n a^\top$, hence a convex polytope whose vertices are the corner matrices $A(v)$ ($v$ a box corner), and whose EXPOSED EDGES are the segments where all coefficients but one are fixed at $\pm$ extremes and one coefficient sweeps its interval.
(2) Relate this to the Edge Theorem framework so the proof 'admits extension to general convex polytopes of matrices' as the problem demands.

**To prove:** the affine-image/convexity claim; the exact vertex and edge sets; and an explicit statement of which steps (boundary-crossing, count-constancy) are GENERAL polytope facts (Edge Theorem) versus which (the four-vertex monotonicity) are SPECIAL to companion form. The solver should either prove the Edge Theorem (exposed-edge sufficiency) or cite Bartlett–Hollot–Huang (1988) with all hypotheses verified.
